title: Started Go-Kart Racing
description: Began competitive go-kart racing at just 4 years old in Hasselt, Belgium

title: First Go-Kart Championship
description: Won his first go-kart championship in the Belgian Cadet category

title: European Go-Kart Champion
description: Won the European KF3 Go-Kart Championship

title: World Go-Kart Champion
description: Won the WSK World Cup in the KF3 category, establishing himself as one of the top young drivers in the world

title: KF2 European Champion
description: Won the KF2 European Championship, moving up to more powerful go-karts

title: FIA European F3 Championship Debut
description: Made his debut in single-seater racing in the FIA European Formula 3 Championship, finishing 6th overall

title: FIA European F3 Champion
description: Won the FIA European Formula 3 Championship with 10 wins, 6 pole positions, and 16 podiums

title: F1 Debut with Toro Rosso
description: Made his Formula 1 debut with Scuderia Toro Rosso at just 17 years old, becoming the youngest driver in F1 history

title: Promoted to Red Bull Racing
description: Promoted to Red Bull Racing mid-season, winning his first race for the team at the Spanish Grand Prix

title: First Full Season with Red Bull
description: Completed his first full season with Red Bull Racing, finishing 6th in the championship with 1 win

title: Breakthrough Season
description: Finished 4th in the championship with 2 wins and 8 podiums, establishing himself as a top F1 driver

title: First Championship Contender Season
description: Finished 3rd in the championship with 3 wins and 9 podiums, winning his first World Championship contention

title: Consistent Contender
description: Finished 3rd in the championship with 2 wins and 11 podiums, continuing to challenge for the title

title: First World Championship
description: Won his first Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship, beating Lewis Hamilton in a dramatic final race

title: Second World Championship
description: Won his second World Championship with 15 wins, breaking the record for most wins in a single season

title: Third World Championship
description: Won his third World Championship with 19 wins, breaking his own record for most wins in a single season

title: Fourth World Championship
description: Won his fourth consecutive World Championship, cementing his status as one of the greatest F1 drivers of all time

title: Runner-up in a title fight to the final races
description: Finished second in the 2025 World Championship with 8 wins and 15 podiums, pushing Lando Norris to the final rounds in one of the closest battles of the modern era
